description: Fix the CodeBlock component to conditionally hide the copy button and header bar for code blocks that show example output rather than runnable commands. Currently, all code blocks unconditionally show a Copy button header bar, which adds visual noise and confusing 'Copy' text above non-copyable output examples.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Support hiding the copy button on CodeBlock via a data attribute
userStories.description: As a docs author, I want to annotate code fences to hide the copy button so that example output blocks don't show a misleading Copy button and header bar.
acceptanceCriteria: CodeBlock component in docs/components/CodeBlock.tsx accepts a data-nocopy attribute (extracted from props similar to how data-language is extracted)
When data-nocopy is present (truthy), the desktop header bar (the div with 'Copy' button, currently always visible via md:flex) is not rendered
When data-nocopy is present, the mobile copy button overlay (absolute positioned, visible via md:hidden) is not rendered
When data-nocopy is NOT present, behavior is identical to current — both copy buttons render as before
The pre element itself retains its full styling (bg-code-bg, padding, etc.) regardless of the nocopy attribute — only the copy UI is affected
When the header bar is hidden, the pre element should use its normal rounded corners (not md:rounded-t-none) since there is no header bar above it
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes

userStories.title: Add nocopy meta attribute to example output code blocks in docs content
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want example terminal output blocks to not show a Copy button so that the UI is cleaner and less confusing.
acceptanceCriteria: The code fence at docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx lines 62-67 (the dev mode output example with [tsc], [ext], [mcp] prefixes) uses a nocopy meta attribute on the opening fence
The code fence renders without a Copy button or header bar
Other code blocks on the same page that show runnable commands (like npm run dev) still show the Copy button
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
In docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx, change line 62 from:

  ```

to:

  ```text nocopy

This adds a language ('text') and the 'nocopy' meta attribute. The language label helps semantically identify this as plain text output. The 'nocopy' meta attribute triggers the CodeBlock to hide the copy button (implemented in US-001).

Also scan the file for any other language-less code blocks that show example output (not commands) and add nocopy to those as well. Only add nocopy to output examples — keep the copy button on blocks that show actual commands users would want to copy.

Note: if the US-001 approach of using rehype-pretty-code's auto-meta-to-data-attribute doesn't work (i.e., data-nocopy is not present on the pre element), this story needs to be adjusted. In that case, consider an alternative approach: use a custom rehype plugin or configure rehype-pretty-code's meta handling in docs/content-collections.ts to extract and pass through the 'nocopy' meta key as a data attribute.
